option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# PWA Debugging Checklist for HRIS Dashboard
## What Was Fixed ✅
1. **Manifest Path**: Changed from `/manifest.webmanifest` → `/manifest.json` (Next.js auto-generates this from `manifest.ts`) 2. **iOS Meta Tags**: Added direct meta tags in `<head>` for Apple devices: - `apple-mobile-web-app-capable` - `apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style` - `apple-mobile-web-app-title` 3. **Android Meta Tags**: Added direct meta tags for Chrome: - `mobile-web-app-capable` - `theme-color` 4. **Manifest Enhancement**: Added `scope`, `categories`, `screenshots`, and better `icons` configuration 5. **Status Bar Style**: Changed from `default` to `black-translucent` for better iOS appearance
---
## How to Test PWA Installation
### **Android Chrome**
1. Open Chrome on your Android device 2. Navigate to your production URL (`https://your-domain.com`) 3. **Wait 5-10 seconds** for the PWA detection 4. You should see one of these: - **Install prompt** at bottom of screen (tap "Install") - **Menu → Install app** option - **Address bar icon** with "Install app" option
**If you don't see it:** - Try Force Refresh: `Ctrl+Shift+R` or menu → "Reload" - Check `chrome://apps` to see if already installed - Ensure HTTPS is working properly
### **iOS Safari (iPhone/iPad)**
1. Open Safari on iOS 2. Navigate to your production URL (`https://your-domain.com`) 3. Tap the **Share button** (box with arrow) 4. Scroll down and tap **"Add to Home Screen"** 5. Confirm the name and tap **"Add"**
**iOS Note:** Safari doesn't auto-prompt like Android. You must manually add via Share menu. This is by design.
---
## Browser DevTools Verification
### **Desktop Chrome/Edge (Simulating Mobile)**
1. Open your production site 2. Press `F12` (DevTools) 3. Click **Hamburger menu → More tools → Application** 4. Go to **Manifest** tab and verify: ``` ✅ name: "HRIS Dashboard" ✅ short_name: "HRIS" ✅ display: "standalone" ✅ start_url: "/" ✅ theme_color: "#0f172a" ✅ scope: "/" ✅ icons: [List of icon definitions] ```
5. Go to **Service Workers** tab and verify: ``` ✅ Status: Running ✅ Scope: https://your-domain.com/ ✅ Updates: "every 1h 24m" or similar ```
6. Go to **Storage → Cache Storage** and verify: ``` ✅ pages-cache (contains cached pages) ✅ images-cache (contains cached images) ✅ workbox-v3-... (Serwist caches) ```
---
## Manifest JSON Endpoint Check
1. Go to browser console and paste: ```javascript fetch('/manifest.json').then(r => r.json()).then(console.log) ```
2. You should see the full manifest object with all properties
---
## iOS Specific Checks
### In Safari DevTools (with Webkit):
1. Check **Source → <head>** for these meta tags: ```html <meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-capable" content="yes" /> <meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style" content="black-translucent" /> <meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-title" content="HRIS" /> ```
2. On actual iOS device: - Install from Safari (Share → Add to Home Screen) - Launch from home screen - It should open in **standalone mode** (no Safari UI) - Status bar should be **black/translucent**
---
## Android Specific Checks
### In Chrome DevTools:
1. **Application → Manifest** tab should show: - `display: "standalone"` ✅ - `start_url: "/"` ✅ - `scope: "/"` ✅ - All icons defined ✅
2. **Application → Service Workers** should show: - Status: **Running** ✅ - Update checked: recent ✅
3. Open DevTools Console: ```javascript navigator.serviceWorker.getRegistrations().then(r => console.log(r[0])) ``` Should show active registration with `/sw.js`
---
## Network Requirements
- ✅ **HTTPS Only** — PWA requires HTTPS (http://localhost is exception) - ✅ **Valid certificate** — Self-signed certificates may work on Chrome but not Safari - ✅ **CORS headers** — If serving from different domain - ✅ **Service Worker accessible** — `/sw.js` must be publicly accessible - ✅ **Manifest endpoint** — `/manifest.json` must be publicly accessible
---
## If Still Not Working
### Step 1: Verify Manifest is Live ```bash curl -I https://your-domain.com/manifest.json # Should return 200 OK ```
### Step 2: Verify Service Worker ```bash curl -I https://your-domain.com/sw.js # Should return 200 OK ```
### Step 3: Check Production Build ```bash cd apps/user-dashboard pnpm build ``` Then inspect `.next/public/` to ensure `sw.js` was generated.
### Step 4: Check Build Environment In `next.config.ts`, Serwist is only enabled in production: ```typescript const isProduction = process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production' // PWA disabled if NODE_ENV !== 'production' ```
**Make sure**: `NODE_ENV=production` when deploying!
### Step 5: Clear Caches & Hard Refresh - Android: Settings → Apps → Chrome → Storage → Clear Cache - iOS: Settings → Safari → Advanced → Website Data (delete site) - Then visit site again in fresh tab
---
## Files Modified
1. ✅ `apps/user-dashboard/src/app/layout.tsx` - Fixed manifest path - Added iOS meta tags - Added Android meta tags
2. ✅ `apps/user-dashboard/src/app/manifest.ts` - Enhanced with scope, categories, screenshots - Better icon configuration
3. ✅ Already present: - `apps/user-dashboard/src/sw.ts` (Serwist service worker) - `apps/user-dashboard/src/components/pwa/pwa-registration.tsx` (SW registration) - `apps/user-dashboard/next.config.ts` (Serwist configuration) - `apps/user-dashboard/public/icons/*` (Icon files)
---
## Final Deployment Steps
```bash # 1. Rebuild pnpm build:user
# 2. Deploy to production (HTTPS) # Make sure NODE_ENV=production
# 3. Test on Android Chrome # - Wait 5-10 seconds for PWA detection # - Look for install prompt
# 4. Test on iOS Safari # - Tap Share → Add to Home Screen # - Verify opens in standalone mode
# 5. Monitor Service Worker Updates # - Browsers check for /sw.js updates hourly # - Users will see update prompts via your PwaRegistration component ```
---
## Troubleshooting Matrix
| Issue | Solution | |-------|----------| | No install prompt on Android | Wait 5-10s, force refresh, check manifest valid | | iOS doesn't show standalone | Check `apple-mobile-web-app-capable: yes` in head | | Service worker not registering | Check `/sw.js` is 200 OK, NODE_ENV=production | | Icons not showing | Verify icon paths exist and are accessible | | Cache not working offline | Check Service Workers tab, ensure NetworkFirst strategy | | Manifest 404 | Verify `manifest.json` endpoint returns 200 OK |
---
## References
- [Web App Manifest MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Manifest) - [iOS PWA Support](https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/AppleApplications/Reference/SafariWebContent/ConfiguringWebApplications/ConfiguringWebApplications.html) - [Android PWA Criteria](https://web.dev/install-criteria/) - [Serwist Documentation](https://serwist.pages.dev/)

option.name: originalContent
updatedContent
originalContent
updatedContent
originalContent
updatedContent
